analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet criticizes the US for denying a visa to Iran’s foreign minister to attend a UN Security Council meeting, asserting the US failed its obligations as UN host and urging it to facilitate attendance. Evaluation: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent) — Applicable. The tweet portrays the US as acting improperly/hostilely by failing its UN host obligations, fitting the depiction of the West (US) as acting against norms. CH2 (China is a benevolent power) — Not applicable; the tweet does not present China’s cooperative or law-abiding role. CH3 (China has an epic history) — Not applicable; no historical or resilience content. CH4 (China has an appropriate political system and honorable values) — Not applicable; no defense of China’s domestic political system or values. CH5 (The government of the Chinese Communist Party succeeds) — Not applicable; no mention of CCP governance or achievements. CH6 (China is interesting culturally/naturally) — Not applicable. Conclusion: Only CH1 is promoted.
